summary: The translation successfully preserves Greek, Hebrew, Latin, and German spans and adheres to the glossary term 'counsel of God', while correctly standardizing most Scripture references into English forms. However, the prose quality check fails due to the retention of Dutch abbreviations such as 'd. i.' and 'blz.' which the rubric explicitly treats as defects, and the review flagging identifies these lapses in standardizing non-English abbreviations.

preserved-language-integrity.details: All Greek, Hebrew, Latin, and German spans from the source are preserved unchanged in the translation.

glossary-adherence.details: The source term 'RAAD GODS' is correctly rendered as 'counsel of God' in the section title and body text.

prose-quality.details: Dutch abbreviations 'd. i.' (dat is) and 'blz.' (bladzijde) were left untranslated instead of being converted to standard English forms 'i.e.' and 'p.'/'pp.', violating the requirement to translate Dutch abbreviations.

review-flagging.details: Findings explicitly identify the untranslated Dutch abbreviations and the inconsistency in rendering them.

findings.detail: The Dutch abbreviation 'd. i.' (dat is) is retained in the translation ('opera essentialia ad intra, d. i. the decrees') instead of being rendered as 'i.e.'; note that the translator correctly used 'i.e.' in later instances, indicating inconsistency.
The Dutch abbreviation 'blz.' (bladzijde) for page numbers is retained in the citation 'Kuenen, Volksgodsd. en wereldgodsd. 1882 blz. 40 v.' instead of being translated to 'p.' or 'pp.'.
The Dutch abbreviation 'v.' (volgende) for 'following' is retained in the citation 'blz. 40 v.' instead of the standard English 'f.' or 'ff.'.

recommended_follow_up: Replace the retained 'd. i.' with 'i.e.' to ensure consistency with the rest of the translation.
Translate 'blz.' to 'p.' and 'v.' to 'f.' or 'ff.' in all bibliographic citations.
Verify that all Dutch bibliographic titles are handled according to project preferences (translated or kept original with translation in brackets).
